etymology.description: Depicts a tree with lush branches on top -> 未
etymology.interpretation: The character 未(mèi, "not yet") depicts a tree 木(木) (mù, "tree") with lush branches on top, representing incompletion or future potential. This image conveys the concept of something not yet ripe or complete, leading to the meaning 'not yet'.

examples.chinese: 我还未听说过这个消息(我還未聽說過這個消息)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ hái mèi tīng shuō guò zhège xiāoxi.
examples.english: I have not yet heard this news.

examples.chinese: 他虽然努力工作，但未完成任务(他雖然努力工作，但未完成任務)。
examples.pinyin: Tā suīrán nǔlì gōngzuò, dàn mèi wánchéng rènwù.
examples.english: Although he worked hard, he did not complete the task.

examples.chinese: 项目未能按时完成(項目未能按時完成)。
examples.english: The project was not completed on time.
